content.body: Halogens are Group VII non-metals: F, Cl, Br, I, At. They have 7 outer electrons and react by gaining one electron to form X⁻ ions.

TREND DOWN GROUP: Colour darkens (F=pale yellow, Cl=yellow-green, Br=orange-brown, I=grey-black vapour/purple in organic). Boiling point increases. Reactivity DECREASES (harder to gain electron as atom gets larger).

DISPLACEMENT REACTIONS: More reactive halogen displaces less reactive one from its salt.
Cl₂ + 2KBr → 2KCl + Br₂ (chlorine displaces bromine)
Cl₂ + 2KI → 2KCl + I₂ (chlorine displaces iodine)

Test for halide ions with AgNO₃:
Cl⁻ → AgCl (white precipitate, soluble in dilute NH₃)
Br⁻ → AgBr (cream precipitate, soluble in conc. NH₃)
I⁻ → AgI (yellow precipitate, insoluble in NH₃)

content.title: Chlorine Uses
content.body:
• Water purification (kills bacteria)
• Making bleach (NaOCl)
• Making PVC and other plastics
• Making HCl

questions.question: Halogens are found in Group:
questions.explanation: Halogens are Group VII (or Group 17) in the periodic table — they have 7 valence electrons.

questions.question: Reactivity of halogens ___ down the group.
questions.explanation: Larger atoms: outer shell further from nucleus, less attraction for incoming electron → less reactive.

questions.question: Chlorine water turns blue litmus red then bleaches it because:
options: Cl₂ dissolves to form HCl (acid) and HOCl (bleach)
questions.answer: Cl₂ dissolves to form HCl (acid) and HOCl (bleach)
questions.explanation: Cl₂ + H₂O → HCl + HOCl. HCl = acid (turns red). HOCl = bleaching agent (decolorizes).

questions.question: Which halogen is a liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Bromine (Br₂) is the only non-metal that is liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: AgNO₃ test: cream precipitate that dissolves in concentrated NH₃ indicates:
questions.explanation: AgBr = cream precipitate, soluble in concentrated but not dilute NH₃. Distinguishes from Cl⁻ and I⁻.

questions.question: Cl₂ + 2NaI → 2NaCl + I₂. This shows:
options: Iodine is more reactive than chlorine
Chlorine is more reactive than iodine
questions.answer: Chlorine is more reactive than iodine
questions.explanation: More reactive halogen (Cl) displaces less reactive one (I) from its salt solution.

questions.question: Iodine vapour appears:
questions.explanation: Iodine vapour (I₂ gas) and solutions in organic solvents are purple/violet. Aqueous iodine is brown.

questions.question: Chlorine is used to purify water because:
questions.explanation: Cl₂ reacts with water to form HOCl (hypochlorous acid), which is a powerful disinfectant.

questions.question: The number of valence electrons in a halogen atom is:
questions.explanation: Group VII = 7 valence electrons. They need 1 more to reach stable octet → form X⁻ ions.

questions.question: Fluorine is the most reactive halogen because:
options: It has the smallest atom with strongest pull on incoming electron
It has the highest boiling point
questions.answer: It has the smallest atom with strongest pull on incoming electron
questions.explanation: Small atom → outer shell close to nucleus → very high attraction for 8th electron → most reactive.

questions.question: Would bromine displace iodine from potassium iodide solution?
questions.explanation: Br is more reactive than I. Br₂ + 2KI → 2KBr + I₂. More reactive displaces less reactive.

questions.question: AgNO₃ test: yellow precipitate insoluble in NH₃ indicates:
questions.explanation: AgI = yellow precipitate, insoluble in both dilute and concentrated NH₃.

questions.question: Bleach is made by reacting chlorine with:
questions.explanation: Cl₂ + 2NaOH → NaCl + NaOCl + H₂O. Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) is the active ingredient in bleach.

questions.question: Iodine solution turns starch:
questions.explanation: Iodine-starch test: iodine trapped in starch helix produces characteristic blue-black colour — identifies starch.

questions.question: The halide ion formed by chlorine is:
questions.explanation: Cl gains 1 electron: Cl + e⁻ → Cl⁻ (chloride ion)

questions.question: Down Group VII, boiling points:
questions.explanation: Larger molecules have stronger van der Waals forces → more energy to separate → higher boiling point.

questions.question: Hydrogen chloride (HCl) gas dissolved in water forms:
questions.explanation: HCl(g) + H₂O → H⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq). This is hydrochloric acid — a strong acid.

questions.question: Which halogen has the highest electronegativity?
questions.explanation: Fluorine is the most electronegative element in the entire periodic table.

questions.question: Would iodine displace bromine from potassium bromide?
questions.explanation: Iodine is LESS reactive than bromine — it cannot displace bromine from its salts.

questions.question: In electrolysis of dilute NaCl solution, which gas is produced at the cathode?
questions.explanation: Cathode (reduction): 2H⁺ + 2e⁻ → H₂. Anode: 2Cl⁻ → Cl₂ + 2e⁻ (in concentrated NaCl).
